title: WHAT COWORK
IS FOR.
segments.body_html: By the end of this chapter you will be able to decide whether a task belongs in Cowork, Claude chat, Claude Code, or hum

beats.text: By the end of this chapter you will be able to decide whether a task belongs in Cowork, Claude chat, Claude Code, or human-only judgment , before you start working.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

segments.section: FOUR TASKS WALK INTO YOUR INBO
title: FOUR TASKS WALK INTO
YOUR INBOX.
segments.body_html: First:→Second:→Third:→Fourth:

beats.text: It is Tuesday morning. You have four things on your list. First: A colleague sent you a draft memo and asked you to make the tone crisper. One file, one pass, done.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · FOUR TASKS WALK INTO YOUR INBO

title: THE FOUR
ROUTES.
segments.body_html: Chat→Cowork→Claude Code→Human-only

beats.text: The book's central routing question: Is this task mainly conversation or thinking? Does it require files, folders, and saved artifacts? Does it require codebase action or command execution? Is it sensitive, regulated, irreversible, or judgment-heavy in a way that requires a human to stay in the chair? Chat is for thinking, drafting, explaining, critiquing, brainstorming, and short source-grounded tasks where you stay close to the output and no files are needed beyond what you paste directly. The memo rewrite is chat.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · THE FOUR ROUTES

title: WHAT MAKES A
TASK COWORK-SHAPED.
segments.body_html:
·Multiple source files that all need to be read
·An output that must be saved rather than just displayed
·More than one or two steps in sequence
·A result you can define in advance and verify afterward
·Source materials in a bounded, accessible folder

beats.text: Cowork fits tasks with the following signals (Anthropic, "Get started with Claude Cowork"): Multiple source files that all need to be read An output that must be saved rather than just displayed More than one or two steps in sequence A result you can define in advance and verify afterward Source materials in a bounded, accessible folder

segments.label: 04 / 13 · WHAT MAKES A TASK COWORK-SHAPE

title: THE ROUTING
MATRIX.
segments.body_html:
Need ideas, draft text, or critique — Chat
Need files assembled into an artifact — Cowork
Need code changes, tests, or a diff — Claude Code
Need final judgment on a person's situation — Human-only

beats.text: This matrix is a starting point, not a formula. Parasuraman, Sheridan, and Wickens (2000) developed a model for types and levels of human interaction with automation that makes a similar point: the right level of automation for a task depends on context, reliability, consequences, and the human's ability to detect errors. Higher automation is not always better automation.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · THE ROUTING MATRIX

title: THE IRONIES OF
ROUTING WRONG.
segments.body_html:
Bainbridge's classic 1983 paper "Ironies of Automation" identified a trap that applies directly to Cowork routing: the m

The Ironies of Routing Wrong — Routing a task to chat when it should be Cowork wastes your time on manual assembly. Routing a task to Cowork when it sh

beats.text: Bainbridge's classic 1983 paper "Ironies of Automation" identified a trap that applies directly to Cowork routing: the more you delegate to automation, the more critical your own skill and attention becomes when the automation fails or reaches its limits. If you route a task to Cowork when it is not ready for Cowork , vague sources, ambiguous goal, no verification path , you do not get a free result. You get a plausible-looking artifact that may be wrong in ways that are hard to catch.

segments.label: 06 / 13 · THE IRONIES OF ROUTING WRONG

title: THINKING THROUGH THE
FOUR CASES.
segments.body_html: Memo rewrite:→Operations packet:→Website script error:→Student accommodation records:

beats.text: Memo rewrite: Chat. Paste the memo, describe the tone, review the reply. If the memo is one document and the task is one pass, there is no workspace to prepare, no artifact to save, and no multi-step plan to review. Cowork would be setup without payoff. Operations packet: Cowork. The task has three source files in separate locations, a structured output format, and a recurring weekly shape that rewards a saved project.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · THINKING THROUGH THE FOUR CASE

segments.section: WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT
title: WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH —
AND THAT IS GOOD
segments.body_html:
·Drafting short documents from materials you paste directly
·Brainstorming, critiquing, and explaining
·Summarizing a single document you can share
·Answering questions with reasoning or analysis
·Revising text with feedback

beats.text: A common mistake is treating Cowork as the more advanced, therefore better, surface. It is not. It is a different surface for a different task shape. Chat is excellent for: Drafting short documents from materials you paste directly Brainstorming, critiquing, and explaining Summarizing a single document you can share Answering questions with reasoning or analysis Revising text with feedback

segments.label: 08 / 13 · WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT

title: HUMAN-ONLY IS
NOT FAILURE.
segments.body_html:
Decisions with real consequences for real peoplepersonnel, accommodations, benefits, legal status
Regulated informationhealth records, student records, financial filings, legal documents
Irreversible actionsdeleting files permanently, sending external communications, making commitments on behalf of others
Ethically ambiguous judgmentsituations where values, context, and relationships matter more than pattern matching

beats.text: The fourth route , human-only , deserves its own defense. In workplaces under pressure to use AI everywhere, it is easy to read "human-only" as "stuck in the past" or "not using the tool well." That reading is wrong. Human-only is the appropriate designation for: Decisions with real consequences for real people: personnel, accommodations, benefits, legal status Regulated information: health records, student records, financial filings, legal documents Irreversible actions: deleting files permanently, sending external communications, making commitments on behalf of others Ethically ambiguous judgment: situations where values, context,

segments.label: 09 / 13 · HUMAN-ONLY IS NOT FAILURE

segments.section: THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER
title: THE HUMAN GATE IN
THIS CHAPTER.
segments.body_html:
·Is this task mainly conversation, or does it require files and a saved artifact?
·Does it require codebase action?
·Does it involve sensitive, regulated, or irreversible consequences?
Is there a clear verification pathcould I check whether the output is right?

beats.text: The routing decision is itself a human judgment. Cowork cannot decide whether a task should be in Cowork. You can. Before opening any session, ask: Is this task mainly conversation, or does it require files and a saved artifact? Does it require codebase action? Does it involve sensitive, regulated, or irreversible consequences? Is there a clear verification path , could I check whether the output is right?

segments.label: 10 / 13 · THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER

title: COMMON MISTAKES
segments.body_html: "Cowork is always better than chat."→"The most powerful surface is the right surface."→"A repetitive task is automatically safe to delegate."→"Code work is just another document task."→"Human-only is a failure to use AI."

beats.text: "Cowork is always better than chat." Cowork has setup cost , workspace, brief, plan, review. For a simple task, that cost exceeds the benefit. "The most powerful surface is the right surface." Routing by power rather than fit is how work gets slower, not faster.

segments.label: 11 / 13 · COMMON MISTAKES
